data.title: Hong Kong’s 36 Stablecoin License Applications: Which One Will Succeed?
data.description: On October 1st, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) disclosed that, as of September 31st, 36 banks, tech giants, and others had submitted applications for stablecoin licenses and entered the HKMA's review process. The first batch of licensees is expected to be announced in early 2026. Publicly available sources indicate that the number of licensees in the first batch is relatively small, with the HKMA capping the number at a single digit (considering the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission's initial issuance of VATP licenses to exchanges, it wouldn't be surprising if only one or two licenses were issued). This means that the vast majority of applicants will fail. Today, the Sajie team will discuss the potential impact of this stablecoin license competition on the industry. 36 companies secured their first victory, but half have failed. While the HKMA announced on October 1st that 36 entities had entered the review process, it did not release the specific names. The main reason for not disclosing this information is that the HKMA has previously emphasized: "Expressions of intent or submissions of stablecoin license applications, as well as the HKMA's communications with relevant institutions, are merely the application process and do not constitute approval of any institution, nor any endorsement of its prospects for obtaining a license." Therefore, to avoid misleading public opinion due to inappropriate publicity by certain entities, the HKMA has withheld the list. Since the release of the Hong Kong Stablecoin Ordinance and the clarification of license application requirements, our team has engaged in discussions with regulators and multiple sources. We have repeatedly emphasized in previous articles that, given the critical role of stablecoins as essential financial infrastructure, only large, well-established, and well-qualified industry giants in finance, technology, and retail have a chance of securing a license during the initial issuance period. Simply put, this is not a game for ordinary businesses. The news released by the HKMA confirms our assumptions. On September 1st, the HKMA revealed that as of August this year, it had received 77 applications for stablecoin licenses. Applications included banks, technology companies, securities/asset management/investment firms, e-commerce companies, payment institutions, and web3 unicorns. Now, in just one month, the number of applicants has (at least) been halved to 36. Combined with the HKMA's previous announcement in September that it had "arranged meetings with institutions expressing interest (in applying for stablecoin licenses), hoping that these discussions will help these institutions carefully assess the necessity and maturity of their stablecoin issuance plans, and ultimately decide whether to submit formal applications," this suggests that the HKMA likely dissuaded half of the applicants during the initial screening phase, including many well-known giants who had initially announced their stablecoin applications with great fanfare. This underscores the fierce competition and high compliance requirements for the initial stablecoin licenses. This reminds us of rumors circulating in the market that a blockchain company under a certain e-commerce company, and a digital technology company under a certain e-commerce company, both withdrew from the first wave of applications for licenses due to excessively high compliance requirements and premature application timing. It now appears that these rumors may be true, and taking a step back might not be a bad idea. After all, sharpening the knife does not delay the chopping of wood. Since the first wave of licenses is already limited, it's better to stand by and observe how others take advantage of this opportunity. Who has the best chance of obtaining the first wave of stablecoin licenses? As mentioned earlier, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority, out of its consistent caution, has limited the number of stablecoin licenses issued in the first wave. So, among the numerous applicants, who has the best chance? We believe the first wave of licensees will likely be large state-owned banks, or combinations of banks, retail businesses, or banks, and tech giants. According to the information disclosed by the HKMA, the applicants for the 36 stablecoin licenses are mainly banks, technology giants, financial institutions and some web3 companies. This structure is also in line with the market's expectations for first-time applicants. After all, the three giants of the stablecoin sandbox have fully demonstrated the selection tendency of Hong Kong regulators. In fact, according to the Stablecoin Ordinance, the application standards for Hong Kong's stablecoin license are not difficult. Even a small and capable company can meet them with effort. The specific requirements are briefly summarized by the Sister Sa team as follows: If we only look at the strength on paper, the Sister Sa team believes that not only the 36 companies, but also the other 41 companies that were persuaded to withdraw are definitely capable of meeting the above requirements. Unfortunately, many rules of the game are not simply written on paper. The reason why the Sister Sa team is optimistic about banks is based on the basic skills of traditional financial institutions in anti-money laundering and 24-hour security monitoring. This is primarily because, in the eyes of regulators, stablecoins aren't simply "products" but rather crucial financial infrastructure crucial to the future of Hong Kong and even my country's financial system. This necessitates that the issuance of licensed stablecoins must prioritize "compliant issuance" rather than "decentralized issuance and autonomous circulation." Therefore, while tech giants like JD.com and Alibaba have ample access to technology and market share, they cannot compare to traditional financial institutions like banks in terms of financial security, particularly in terms of extensive and sophisticated know-your-customer (KYC) processes, anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing, and 24/7 monitoring. Furthermore, the relevant HKMAs are, after all, the "parents" of traditional financial institutions like banks. They are intimately familiar with them and enjoy a strong foundation of trust. Tech giants currently feel somewhat like "other people's children," making it difficult to convince them in the short term. Financial security is a matter of great importance, and it takes time to truly understand people's opinions. Of course, there are shortcuts for tech giants to quickly enter the market, and forming a consortium is one viable option. Take the consortium of Standard Chartered Bank (Hong Kong) Limited, Animoca Brands, and Hong Kong Telecom (HKT) as an example. The three-party consortium has successfully entered the Hong Kong Stablecoin Sandbox for testing. Standard Chartered, of course, is one of Hong Kong's three major note-issuing banks and possesses robust anti-money laundering compliance. HKT's partners are likely familiar with the company, a renowned telecommunications infrastructure provider in Hong Kong, providing basic services like local calls and broadband access to nearly 90% of Hong Kong residents. Animoca Brands may be less well-known to the public, but it began as a mobile game developer specializing in IP. Early on, it built a reputation by collaborating with many globally renowned IPs. Around 2000, during the height of the NFT and GameFi crazes, it entered the Web3 blockchain gaming market and achieved significant success. This three-in-one consortium addresses regulatory requirements, including financial security, market, technology, and user satisfaction. It's no surprise that it was the first entity to enter the Stablecoin Sandbox. Finally, the Sajie team would like to clarify two concepts for partners: stablecoin licenses and the stablecoin sandbox. Many partners mistakenly believe that entering the stablecoin sandbox is a prerequisite for obtaining a license, or that entering the sandbox guarantees a license. In fact, both of these views are incorrect. The Hong Kong Monetary Authority positions the sandbox as a "window for dialogue between regulators and the market in the early stages of stablecoin development"—in layman's terms, akin to a guinea pig. Therefore, the HKMA has long made it clear that there is no direct causal relationship between the sandbox and a license. Entering the sandbox does not guarantee a license, nor does it even increase the probability of obtaining a license. Ultimately, it comes down to who has the most compliant and sustained compliance in their final plan. Of course, the three companies in the sandbox certainly have a first-mover advantage. After all, while others are still in the design phase, these three have already launched their coins for testing. It is highly likely that one of them will be granted a license in the future.

data.title: Logical analysis of the start, operation, and end of the BTC market in this cycle
data.description:
According to Coinbase, BTC reached a four-year low of $15,460.00 per coin on November 21, 2022. We consider this date the end of the previous cycle and the beginning of this one. From that date to September 30th of this year, BTC has been in turmoil for 1,044 days, approaching the peak of the previous two cycles (approximately 1,060 days after the low point). Using a similar approach, BTC will reach its current cycle peak in October 2025. Comparison of BTC Price Trends Over Five Cycles: BTC's "cyclical" behavior stems from the speculative frenzy fueled by the spread of consensus and supply cuts. To this day, it remains the most valued cyclical indicator for traditional large BTC holders. This group played a decisive role in shaping past BTC peaks. It was this group's frantic profit-taking and selling, draining liquidity and ultimately forging the market's cyclical peak. Currently, this group is accelerating their selling, suggesting a peak is imminent. However, other indicators of a top, such as a sharp rise in prices and a surge in new addresses, have not appeared. This raises questions: will this "cyclical law" continue to suppress the market and establish a peak, or will it expire? Will the BTC bull market that began in November 2022 end in October?

In this report,EMC Labs uses its self-developed "BTC Cycle Multi-Factor Analysis Model" to conduct a comprehensive analysis of BTC price trends since this cycle, clarifying which market forces and underlying logic have truly driven the cycle forward, and ultimately providing our analysis and judgment on whether BTC prices will peak in October.

The first phase (

2022.11~2023.09): Long-term holdings

Looking back at history, one of the main buyers in the previous cycle

Statistics of changes in long-hand positions in the previous cycle

As in previous cycles, long-handed investors began to accumulate chips during the bear market phase of the previous cycle. As the market entered its bottoming phase, short-term loss-making selling began to taper off, and long-term buying power began to translate into upward price momentum, pushing BTC and the crypto market out of its trough and into a new cycle. Meanwhile, the post-pandemic Federal Reserve's interest rate hike cycle began to draw to a close, officially ending on July 26, 2023. Due to forward trading, the Nasdaq Composite Index bottomed out on October 13, 2022, and emerged from its bottoming range in January 2023. BTC prices largely tracked this trend, approximately 9-10 months before the official halt in interest rate hikes. As the interest rate hike cycle nears its end, tightening monetary policy has led to bankruptcies at regional US banks (Silicon Valley Bank and First Republic Bank), forcing the US government to urgently release liquidity. The US M2/DXY index has begun to bottom out and rebound, providing an external environment for a bottoming-out rebound in the US stock market and Bitcoin.

United States M2/DXY

We will

In the first stage, BTC rebounded from a low of $15,476.00 to a high of $31,862.21, with a maximum increase of 105.88%.

Phase II (2023.10~2024.03): BTC Spot ETF

U.S. inflation continued to decline. The brief rebound in CPI from July to September 2023 was considered a false alarm, and July was finally confirmed as the end month of the Fed's current round of interest rate hikes.

As market expectations change, risky assets begin to be favored by funds. The change in risk appetite has prepared BTC for the launch of the second phase of the market. What really drives BTC to start the second stage of this cycle is the expected approval of the BTC Spot ETF and the fifth BTC halving in April 2024.

U.S. CPI

BlackRock, Fidelity and other traditional Wall Street asset management giants submitted BTC Spot ETF applications to the SEC in June 2023, and forward-looking speculative trading funds were secretly gathering.

Taking SEC approval of BTC Spot ETF on January 10, 2024 as the dividing line, the second stage of the market is divided into the first and second halves. The first half (2023.10~2024.01.10) is dominated by speculative funds betting on the approval of the ETF, and the second half (2024.01.10~2024.03.14) is dominated by incremental funds (over US$12 billion) brought by the ETF channel.

Monthly statistics of BTC Spot ETF and stablecoin channel fund flows

In addition, the stablecoin channel also completely got rid of the outflow trend in October and resumed inflow. By the end of March, the total new issuance exceeded US$26 billion, which was one of the main driving forces in the first half. Since the start of this phase of the market in October 2024, long-term investors began to reduce their holdings, and by the end of the market, the scale of reduction reached 900,000 coins. This phase of the market was jointly dominated by BTC Spot ETF channel speculation/investment funds, on-site speculation/investment funds (manifested in a large amount of stablecoin issuance) and long-term investors reducing their holdings. Buying power outweighs selling power, and BTC prices rise sharply, and the market is very fierce.

In the second stage,BTC rose from a low of $26,955.25 to a high of $73,835.57, a maximum increase of 173.92%. With the Trump campaign, massive amounts of funds were rapidly injected into the crypto market through ETFs and stablecoin channels, forming the largest inflow of funds so far in this cycle. At the same time, long-term investors once again started selling to lock in profits. Driven by the crypto-friendly policies of the United States, crypto assets have gradually become mainstream assets in the United States. In addition to BTC Spot ETF, dozens of DATs companies represented by Strategy have joined the battle to hoard BTC and other crypto assets. These two groups have become the largest buyers of BTC. BTC Spot ETFs and DATs companies now hold over or close to 5% of BTC. With the massive entry of BTC Spot ETFs and DATs, BTC is experiencing a period of significant turnover. Large amounts of BTC are being transferred from early holders into custodial accounts held by BTC Spot ETFs and DATs. This has caused a significant decline in BTC held on centralized exchanges, commonly used by early crypto holders. As of the end of September 2025, more than 400,000 BTC had flowed out of the management addresses of centralized exchanges, which is worth more than $40 billion at $100,000.

BTC inventory statistics on major cryptocurrency exchanges

During this stage and after, this outflow continued, which shows that

BTC is currently in a historic turnover. Early investors (including those who have held for more than seven years) are cashing out with huge profits, while traditional funds are shifting to long-term investment in this asset. The behavior of early investors is significantly influenced by the halving cycle, while DATs companies appear to favor continuous buying and long-term holding. The behavior of holders of the BTC Spot ETF channel is more influenced by US stock market trends. This shift in the holding structure complicates the shaping of BTC's cycles.

This period of market momentum came from speculation driven by interest rate cuts and expectations of Trump's crypto-friendly policies. The crypto market received record capital inflows during this period.

In the fourth stage,

BTC prices rose from a low of $63,301.25 to $109,358.01 (recorded on January 20, 2025, the day Trump took office), with a maximum increase of 72.76%.

The Fifth Stage (

2025.02~2025.04): Black Swan<span yes';mso-ansi-font-weight:bold;font-size:12.0000pt;">

In our research framework, the fifth stage is another mid-term adjustment caused by the combination of an external black swan event and a resurgence of sentiment following passionate speculation. The market turmoil brought about by the suspension of interest rate cuts and the tariff war reached a threshold in both time and space, ultimately forming this special stage.

Monthly Statistics of Capital Flows in the Crypto Market

Behind the V-shaped recovery, funds from DATs companies, BTC Spot ETF channels, and stablecoin channels rushed to buy shares. In addition, long-term investors returned to increase their holdings in a timely manner after the decline, once again playing the role of a market stabilizer.

The fifth stage, the highest price $73,777, the lowest price $49,000, the maximum drop of 33.58%, did not exceed the scale of BTC bull market correction.

The sixth stage (2025.05~): Old cycle and new cycle

The market plunge caused by the black swan event was gradually recovered by bargain-hunting funds and long-term holdings. By July, BTC had reached a record high of $123,000. At this point, long-term investors initiated the third major sell-off of this cycle, which continues to this day. The investors were the DATs and BTC Spot ETF channel funds. In leaf="">Before the September rate cut, forward-looking trading continued to dominate the market. From July to September, funds inflowed significantly, but the scale of inflow gradually decreased, which led to a slight adjustment in BTC after the rate cut. Long-term reduction of holdings became the main activity affecting market movements.

Statistics of changes in BTC long-term holdings

Bitcoin Network New Address Statistics

But when observing the largest SCP platform Ethereum, we can notice that the same situation has occurred in the new addresses added in this cycle.

Ethereum New Address Statistics

This makes us have to believe that, The BTC market structure has undergone dramatic changes, and the entire crypto market is entering a new stage of development. Simply predicting market tops based on cyclical patterns, or blindly purchasing cryptocurrencies in pursuit of hot spots and expecting high returns, are outdated. It's even possible that BTC has already exited its old cycle and entered a new one, potentially radically altering the manner and timing of its peak and the magnitude of any bear market correction. Conclusion From the above review and observation, we have come to a preliminary conclusion: the driving force of this bull market mainly comes from the promotion of industrial policies and incremental funds from traditional channels. Production cuts and industrial innovation have failed to bring about huge capital inflows as in the past, thereby triggering a comprehensive bull market in the Crypto market with all the coins soaring. Although during this round of bull market, the industry has also experienced There have been innovations in niche areas such as Ordinals, Restaking, Solana Renaissance, and DePhin, but compared to the previous ICO and DeFi craze, the funds attracted by these innovations are pulsed and extremely limited.

This has resulted in the prices of most coins and tokens in the crypto market only experiencing pulsed, phased increases since BTC restarted its new bull market in November 2022. Even ETH, the SCP platform token with the largest consensus and the most use cases, saw its price fall back to the starting point of the bull market in 2025.

BTC is moving out of the old cycle and into a new one. DATs companies and BTC Spot Driven by market sentiment and its own logic, funds in the ETF channel are attempting to reshape the logic and shape of the cycle. However, the group of long-term Bitcoin holders, who have played a decisive role in the cyclical movement over the past 16 years, still holds over 15 million BTC, representing 70% of all issued BTC, and this group continues to act according to the law of the cycle. Factors supporting the possibility that the market has not yet peaked or even entered a new cycle include: DATs' outstanding fundraising capabilities and long-term holding strategies, the continued introduction and implementation of crypto-friendly policies in the United States, and the trend towards high-risk asset allocation triggered by the resumption of the interest rate cut cycle. Will long-term investors diligently drain liquidity to complete the old cycle's top, or will buying power in an interest rate cut environment bury selling pressure and usher in a new, long-term bull market for US stocks? This game is still ongoing.

We tend to believe the cycle will be extended.

There's still a low probability that BTC will peak in October, but if long-term investors persist in selling, the bull market's end this year is highly likely. The length and scope of the bear market correction following the bull market could be significantly reduced, depending on the behavior of new buyers.

data.title: Bitcoin Jesus Roger Ver Walks Away From US Tax Case After $48 Million Agreement
data.description:
Bitcoin Jesus Nears $48 Million Settlement To End US Tax Fraud Case

Roger Ver, one of the earliest and most influential advocates of Bitcoin, is on the brink of closing one of the most politically charged legal battles in the crypto world.

The 46-year-old entrepreneur, known widely as “Bitcoin Jesus,” has struck a provisional agreement with the US Department of Justice (DOJ) that could see tax fraud charges against him dropped in exchange for a $48 million settlement.

JUST IN: 🇺🇸 DOJ agrees to drop fraud and tax evasion charges against Roger Ver as part of a settlement requiring a $48 million payment - NYT. pic.twitter.com/ulPtTVXM2W

— Whale Insider (@WhaleInsider) October 9, 2025

Tentative Deal Could End High-Profile Case

According to sources familiar with the matter, Ver has reached a deferred-prosecution agreement with US authorities.

Under its terms, he would pay the $48 million in unpaid taxes tied to his digital asset holdings.

If approved, the agreement would lead to the dismissal of the criminal case after compliance with the terms.

The Justice Department has yet to file the agreement in court, and the deal remains subject to change.

Its finalisation would come amid a broader shift in Washington’s approach to digital assets under President Donald Trump’s administration.

From Bitcoin Evangelist To Tax Fraud Defendant

Ver’s legal troubles stem from a 2024 indictment accusing him of tax evasion and filing a false return.

US prosecutors alleged he concealed 131,000 Bitcoin through companies he controlled — MemoryDealers and Agilestar — and failed to declare gains from the eventual sale of $240 million worth of crypto in 2017.

Roger Ver, 45 , who renounced his USA citizenship in 2014 , is arrested in Spain. The USA government arrested Roger Ver for selling bitcoin for $240 million and not reporting the sell to IRS - internal USA agency that targets opponents of the ruling regime.

Roger was critical…

— Valentine Goikhman (@VGoikhman) May 1, 2024

The case also focused on an “exit tax” the government claimed Ver avoided paying after renouncing his US citizenship in 2014.

The DOJ argued that he underreported the true value of his holdings at the time, depriving the country of tens of millions in tax revenue.

Ver has denied any wrongdoing, framing the charges as politically driven.

His legal team described the case as part of a broader campaign under the previous administration to “weaponise” enforcement against the crypto sector.

Arrest In Spain Sparked Political Firestorm

In February 2024, Spanish authorities arrested Ver at a crypto conference in Barcelona at the request of US officials.

#Bitcoin Roger Ver has been arrested in Spain for extradition to the United States. Full indictment: https://t.co/kwoUr2efpVpic.twitter.com/bZn4N1L9Xf

— Richard Heart (@RichardHeartWin) April 30, 2024

Washington sought his extradition to face trial in Los Angeles.

Ver challenged the extradition, filing a case at the European Court of Human Rights, accusing Spain of breaching his legal protections.

In a video posted online earlier this year, Ver said he faced a possible 100-year sentence if convicted.

Directly appealing to the White House, he wrote on X,

“Mr. President, I am an American, and I need your help. Only you, with your commitment to justice, can save me.”

Mr. President, I am an American, and I need your help. Only you, with your commitment to justice, can save me @realDonaldTrumppic.twitter.com/WhVTZ1M1GB

— Roger Ver (@rogerkver) January 26, 2025

Crypto Industry Pushed For Clemency

The case quickly became a rallying point for crypto leaders who argued Ver’s prosecution was excessive.

In March, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin weighed in on the case, arguing that authorities may have targeted Ver because of his outspoken views on personal freedom.

He compared the situation to Ross Ulbricht’s case, suggesting the government should prioritise recovering unpaid taxes over pursuing severe criminal penalties.

Going to prison for the rest of your life over non-violent tax offenses is absurd. The case against Roger seems very politically motivated; like with @RealRossU, there have been plenty of people and corporations who have been accused of far worse and yet faced sentences far… https://t.co/7G3zDkn2F2

— vitalik.eth (@VitalikButerin) March 1, 2025

Ulbricht, founder of the Silk Road marketplace, was sentenced to double life imprisonment before receiving a presidential pardon from Trump earlier this year.

Shortly after Trump’s inauguration, Elon Musk — who at the time headed the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — publicly committed to reviewing clemency options for Ver.

Ties To Trump Allies Strengthened Ver’s Defence

Ver positioned himself close to Trump’s political network during the legal battle.

In April, reports revealed he paid $600,000 to Republican strategist Roger Stone to lobby against the tax provisions at the heart of the case.

He also hired prominent attorneys Christopher Kise and Brian Ballard, both well-connected in Trump’s circle.

Roger Stone getting pardons for crypto jesus and bitmex crypto scammer? According to WSJ Roger Ver paid Stone $600,000 to hoping to get a pardon when Trump took office in 2024. https://t.co/JWnRwIcxcv

— American Summer - Jesse P. (@americans12499) June 30, 2025

His legal manoeuvres appeared to align with a larger political shift.

The Trump administration has softened its stance on crypto enforcement, reversing several aggressive measures implemented under the previous government.

Washington’s Enforcement Strategy Shifts Under Trump

Ver’s deal follows a series of moves signalling a retreat from hardline crypto regulation.

Earlier this year, the SEC dropped multiple lawsuits against digital asset firms, including Coinbase.

Ross Ulbricht’s pardon and the clemency granted to BitMEX’s founders further highlighted the administration’s new approach.

Ver has long tied his case to this broader political shift, claiming his prosecution was emblematic of the previous administration’s hostility toward crypto.

A Controversial Figure In Crypto History

Roger Ver remains one of the most polarising figures in the industry.

His early evangelism — when he handed out Bitcoin for free to raise awareness — earned him his famous nickname.

In later years, he became a vocal supporter of Bitcoin Cash, often clashing with Bitcoin maximalists.

A former felon who served time in 2002 for selling explosives online, Ver renounced his US citizenship in 2014 and became a citizen of St Kitts and Nevis.

If the agreement with the DOJ is finalised, it would close one of the most closely watched legal cases involving a crypto pioneer — a case that blends money, politics, and the shifting regulatory landscape shaping the industry today.

data.title: The Three Fires of ZK Narrative
data.description: If the ZK narrative fully explodes, it will ignite at least three fires: Privacy → Scaling → General. 1. ZK-Privacy. ZK-Privacy achieves transaction anonymity through zero-knowledge proofs. Its core logic is to hide details such as the sender, receiver, and amount in a transaction. This not only responds to long-standing concerns about privacy protection but also demonstrates strong demand in scenarios such as enhancing Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance and protecting data for large AI model training. The recent resurgence of Zcash is driven by both institutional interest and endorsement by prominent investors who see it as "an insurance policy for Bitcoin." More importantly, Zcash's optional privacy features strike a balance between compliance and anonymity, making it a core element of the current privacy narrative. Key targets for observation: $ZEC  $XMR $DASH $XVG $ARRR 2. ZK-Scaling (Extension) ZK-Scaling, primarily based on ZK-Rollup technology, provides a high-throughput, low-cost Layer 2 scaling solution for blockchains like Ethereum. In the past two cycles, ZK's market hype stemmed from its "end-game application" in Layer 2 scenarios. However, the challenge now is that general Layer 2 infrastructure is already quite sufficient, and whether the ecosystem can keep pace is the decisive factor. Starknet has benefited from the surge in the privacy sector this round. Its founder, Eli Ben-Sasson, was a core member of the Zcash team in his early years. As the privacy craze wanes, it remains to be seen whether ZK scaling can take over as the second wave of the main upward trend. Key targets for observation: $STRK  $ZK $LRC $IMX 3. ZK-General The goal of ZK-General is to generalize zero-knowledge proof technology, enabling its embedding in a wider range of verticals, including cross-chain bridges, private smart contracts, identity verification, and AI reasoning. The core focus is to reduce the cost of use and promote the large-scale commercialization of ZK technology. Currently, projects such as Mina, Aleo, Boundless, and Succinct are actively developing in this direction. Logically, generalization should be the first wave of ZK technology's explosion, followed by the logical extension of scalability and privacy. However, the reality is quite the opposite—institutional power and policy direction appear to have significantly reshaped the pace of technology dissemination. Key projects to watch: $ZKC  $PROVE $MINA $ALEO $AZTEC  $VFY $IRON Additionally, several projects exist at the intersection of privacy and scalability. Railgun is committed to building a privacy layer on public chains like Ethereum, providing a complete privacy solution through application protocols; Horizen is positioned as a platform supporting the development of privacy-focused applications and promoting the large-scale adoption of "ZK as a Service." These projects have risen in tandem with Zcash, both due to their real TVL and usage data at the application layer and due to their smaller market capitalizations and limited name recognition, resulting in greater price resilience.

data.title: When Money Goes Programmable — Benefits Go Viral
data.description:
I've been thinking about how we divide the world into different layers. We like to think of money as one thing, infrastructure as another, and distribution as something completely different. This is clearer and simplistic. A dollar is a dollar, a bank is a bank, and a payment network is a payment network. But none of this is true. Money is always inextricably linked to the systems that circulate it, and the systems that circulate it are always tied to who controls its use. Every time money moves through the system, those who control it profit from it. We just pretend otherwise because it's convenient. What's happening now isn't that cryptocurrencies are blurring these lines; it's that these lines never existed in the first place, and everyone is just realizing it now. For me, this illusion has finally been shattered, and it stems from a seemingly mundane factor: interest rates. Your bank savings account only offers a meager 0.4% interest rate, while a stablecoin backed by the same government bonds offers 4% to 5%. This clearly demonstrates some kind of market failure. Clearly, what we call "money" and what we call "infrastructure" are never separate entities. Banks offer such low interest rates not because banking costs are high, but because they can. They control both the funds and the channels, and they've decided to keep the interest rate differential for themselves. The issuer creates the currency. The blockchain transfers the currency. The platform distributes the currency. For a brief moment, we could clearly see each layer and price them independently. Once people could see the layers clearly, they realized that whoever controlled one would lose profit. The rational approach was to control all of them. Now everyone is scrambling to rebuild the technical architecture, fearing someone else will get there first. For years, stablecoins were simple. Tether and Circle dominated the market with USDT and USDC, creating a seemingly unbreakable duopoly. Together, they controlled 90% of the stablecoin market. Their business model was straightforward. They held customer funds in Treasury bonds, yielding 4% to 5% annually, paid no interest to customers, and pocketed the difference. In 2024, Tether earned $13 billion, and Circle had $1.7 billion in revenue. Not bad for a company that essentially operated like a money market fund and didn't share profits. But then the question is: what if we distributed yields to holders? Since the end of 2024, the market share of yield-generating stablecoins has tripled, reaching over $14 billion in market capitalization and currently accounting for over 6% of the total stablecoin market. JPMorgan analysts predict that if this growth continues, yield-generating stablecoins could account for as much as half of the market. If Tether holds a 5%-yielding Treasury bond and you hold 0%-yielding USDT, then theoretically, for every $100 you hold, someone else is earning $5. If this number is expanded to hundreds of billions of dollars, it represents one of the most significant transfers of wealth in the digital age, flowing silently from holders to issuers. Banks Aware of Competition At the Blockchain Summit in Washington, D.C., Senator Kirsten Gillibrand articulated the banking industry's concerns, which view interest-bearing stablecoins as an existential threat. "Do you want stablecoin issuers to pay interest? Probably not, because if they did, you wouldn't have to keep your money in your local bank." The U.S. Treasury Department calculated that allowing interest-bearing stablecoins could drain $6.6 trillion from bank deposits. Standard Chartered Bank goes further, estimating that emerging market banks alone could lose $1 trillion over the next three years. In countries experiencing double-digit inflation, stablecoins aren't speculative. They're a survival mechanism, a way to hold onto dollars when local currencies depreciate. So the banks did what any rational incumbent would do. They lobbied for the GENIUS Act, which passed in July 2025 and explicitly prohibited stablecoin issuers from paying yield or interest to holders. Problem solved, right? Not quite. The GENIUS Act prohibited issuers from paying interest, but made no mention of intermediaries. Circle quickly realized this. Instead of paying interest directly to USDC holders, Circle shared reserve revenue with Coinbase through a business partnership. Coinbase then used this money to pay USDC rewards to its customers. From an economic perspective, holders received at least some of the proceeds. From a legal perspective, it was Coinbase, not Circle, that made the payments. The arrangement wasn't even subtle. Circle and Coinbase publicly described it as a revenue-sharing model, and in 2024, they jointly generated $1.7 billion in revenue. Other platforms quickly followed suit. PayPal, through a similar partnership with issuer Paxos, offers balance rewards for PYUSD. This income doesn't come from thin air. It comes from the same Treasury bonds that have always backed these stablecoins, simply through an additional intermediary. The Bank Policy Institute is understandably unhappy. They are urging Congress to end the so-called "interest payment workaround" by prohibiting indirect payments by affiliates and agents. Whether regulators will take action depends on how the final rule interprets "interest payment" and whether intermediaries will be categorized with issuers. Currently, this loophole remains in place, and trading volume is significant. For Tether and Circle, the solution has always been liquidity and ubiquity. USDT and USDC are widely accepted, integrated by all major exchanges, and embedded in all DeFi protocols. Their network effects have always been their defensive moat. But that moat is beginning to crumble. While banks and regulators quibble over semantics, something more fundamental is happening. Tether and Circle's combined market share peaked at 91.6% in March 2024. By the end of 2025, it had fallen to 86% and continues to decline. This isn't just due to the availability of yield-generating stablecoins; it's also due to the significantly lower cost and ease of issuing them. A few years ago, launching a white-label stablecoin meant calling Paxos and incurring high fixed costs. Today, you have options like Anchorage, Brale, M0, Agora, or Stripe's Bridge. Companies in the Galaxy Digital portfolio have already launched stablecoins at the seed stage using Bridge's infrastructure. The barrier to entry has been significantly lowered. Exchanges, wallets, and DeFi protocols realized they no longer needed to rely on USDC or USDT. They could issue their own stablecoins, internalize the returns, and return some of those returns to their users. Bridge co-founder Zach Abrams articulated the situation clearly: "If you use an off-the-shelf stablecoin to build a neobank, you can't fully achieve the returns you need to create an optimal savings account. Your reserve mix can't be customized. And you have to pay a 10 basis point redemption fee to withdraw your funds." So they stopped using off-the-shelf stablecoins. The popular Solana wallet Phantom recently launched Phantom Cash, a Bridge-issued stablecoin with built-in yield and debit card functionality. Hyperliquid launched an open bid for its own stablecoin, aiming to reduce its reliance on USDC and generate reserve yield for the protocol. Ethena has been particularly successful in marketing its revenue-sharing model to exchanges. MetaMask, the leading self-custodial Ethereum wallet, has also entered the stablecoin space. In partnership with Bridge and M0, MetaMask launched MetaMask USD (mUSD), integrating it directly into its wallet for on-chain use, exchange, and, soon, debit card payments. Thanks to MetaMask's in-wallet "stablecoin yield" feature, integrated with the Aave mining pool, users can now earn passive income not only on mUSD, but also on major stablecoins like USDC, USDT, and DAI. Other exchanges are also joining forces to form alliances. The Global Dollar Network, powered by Paxos, includes major players like Robinhood, Kraken, Anchorage, Galaxy, and Bullish. Every $1 billion in USDT deposited by investors generates approximately $50 million in annual funding for Tether. You provide trading infrastructure, custody, liquidity, regulatory compliance, and customer support. Tether provides the token. Guess who gets that $50 million? Blockchains want stablecoins, and stablecoin issuers want blockchains. This convergence works both ways. Hyperliquid holds approximately $6 billion in USDC. If all this activity were conducted on Hyperliquid's native stablecoin, USDH, both reserve earnings and transaction fees would flow back into the ecosystem for buybacks and growth. This is the sustainable revenue stream Circle currently controls. Following the success of USDH, other Layer 1 protocols have followed suit. Projects like Ethena are offering a "stablecoin-as-a-service" model, enabling the ecosystem to deploy compliant and yield-generating stablecoins without shouldering heavy technical or regulatory burdens. Stablecoin issuers are now launching their own chains. Why? Because running on external chains creates dependencies: performance issues, throughput bottlenecks, fees, wallets, and third-party cross-chain bridges. Each touchpoint introduces friction and, more importantly, value leakage. By launching their own chain, stablecoin issuers can vertically integrate the currency and settlement layers, taking control of both value and user experience. Circle's Arc is the most obvious example. Circle built Arc to run USDC with zero fees and instant settlement, and then created CCTP as the official cross-chain bridge for burning and minting native USDC on different chains, rather than wrapping it. Circle now controls this chain and all cross-chain flows.

Tether then launched Plasma, a new Layer 1 blockchain built specifically for stablecoin payments, powered by but not limited to USDT. Plasma's design focuses on enabling ultra-fast, zero-fee transfers of stablecoins, while removing unnecessary features from general-purpose chains. Its bridge, USDT0, currently handles $8 billion in trading volume, exceeding CCTP and Wormhole combined. What began as infrastructure for transferring tokens has evolved into a way to control liquidity and remove middlemen from capturing value between chains. Institutions are building entire technology stacks, and Stripe clearly demonstrates where this trend is headed. Stripe processes $1.05 trillion in transactions annually. Instead of building on Ethereum or Solana, it launched its own payment-optimized chain, Tempo. Why? Because at this scale, Stripe can't afford the congestion or governance risks associated with other chains. Tempo integrates with Bridge (for issuing stablecoins) and Privy (for wallets), providing a complete architecture: chain, coin, and custody. What strikes me most is that this doesn't seem like a disruption, but more like a revelation. Technology doesn't change the rules; it reveals them for what they are. Control of money is control of infrastructure, control of access. These were never three different things. They were one thing, just wearing three different masks. The optimistic view is that once everyone understands this, competition will improve the system. Perhaps a dozen vertically integrated architectures competing against each other will produce better results than just one. Perhaps regulatory clarity around stablecoins will prevent the worst abuses. Perhaps the costs of building and maintaining a complete architecture will naturally limit the ability of any single player to extract value. The pessimistic view, however, is that we're simply witnessing the same consolidation game unfolding faster and more visibly. The winners won't be those with more advanced technology or fairer economic models. They will be the ones who reestablish their monopoly fastest before anyone else.

One ​​way or another, the illusion is gone. The next decade will tell us whether it matters or not.

data.title: When Decentralization Goes Dark: Afghanistan Blackout Exposes Web3’s Fragile Core
data.description:
Afghanistan’s 48-Hour Internet Blackout Exposes Blockchain’s Centralization Paradox

Afghanistan plunged into digital darkness last week as a 48-hour nationwide internet blackout revealed one of blockchain’s most overlooked weaknesses — its dependence on centralized internet infrastructure.

The shutdown, which began in late September, left around 13 million Afghans offline and silenced virtually all forms of digital communication. While officials under Taliban rule claimed “technical issues” with fiber optic cables, international reports pointed to a deliberate government-ordered shutdown — the first nationwide blackout since the Taliban regained control.

Three weeks ago, the Taliban cut the internet across the entire country, leaving 40 million people in the dark.

Afghans using Starlink are risking their lives to stay connected with the world.

Yet, in our upside-down reality, they are being praised. pic.twitter.com/A2x2nOcrFq

— Meshy (@meshygrey) October 7, 2025

Beyond cutting citizens off from information, the incident sent shockwaves through the Web3 community, exposing a painful truth: even the most decentralized blockchains remain useless without access to the centralized systems that power the internet itself.

A Wake-Up Call for Decentralization

“The Afghanistan blackout is not just a regional connectivity crisis — it is a wake-up call,” said Michail Angelov, co-founder of Roam Network, a decentralized WiFi platform. “When connectivity is monopolized by a handful of providers, the promise of blockchain can collapse overnight.”

The episode underscored an uncomfortable irony for blockchain advocates: technologies built to resist censorship still rely on infrastructure that can be shut down at will. Despite years of innovation, most users connect to blockchains through internet service providers controlled by governments or large telecoms — the same gatekeepers blockchain was meant to outgrow.

Off-Grid Digital Payments without Mobile Connectivity” using the XRPL and ILP.👇 https://t.co/4gID3PyEXMpic.twitter.com/GwBDtkn8XE

— SMQKE (@SMQKEDQG) April 23, 2025

Afghanistan isn’t alone. Similar digital blackouts have struck other nations grappling with unrest or censorship. In June, the Iranian government restricted global internet access for nearly two weeks amid escalating regional tensions, allowing only state-approved domestic apps to operate. Millions of Iranians resorted to hidden proxy links just to stay online.

These shutdowns illustrate how easily centralized control over the internet can undermine blockchain’s core promises of global access and financial autonomy.

DePIN Projects Aim to Fill the Connectivity Gap

In response, developers are accelerating efforts to decentralize not just finance but also physical infrastructure through DePIN (Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Networks). These projects aim to distribute internet access the same way blockchains distribute data — across many nodes rather than a few corporate servers.

Roam, for instance, is building a smartphone-powered wireless network that crowdsources signal data to create a live, self-updating “connectivity map.” Its upcoming eSIM technology will allow devices to automatically switch between public carriers, private mesh networks, and peer-to-peer local connections — potentially bypassing centralized choke points during outages.

📖Definition of Roam: An Open Wireless Network powered by OpenRoaming WiFi & Smart Global eSIM.

It’s more than connectivity, it’s a new revolution. pic.twitter.com/1NbMwe7FaJ

— Roam (@weRoamxyz) October 2, 2025

Other players are also scaling quickly. World Mobile, the largest decentralized wireless network, reports 2.3 million daily active users across more than 20 countries. Meanwhile, Helium Network boasts over 112,000 mobile hotspots in 190 countries, rewarding individuals with HNT tokens for providing coverage.

For Web3’s grand vision of censorship resistance to succeed, advocates argue that decentralization must extend beyond the blockchain itself. Without decentralized access to the internet, even the most secure distributed ledgers remain vulnerable to the flip of a switch.

As Angelov warned, “If decentralization stops at the protocol layer, we haven’t really solved the problem — we’ve just shifted where the control lies.”

A Revolution Still Plugged Into the Old World

Afghanistan’s blackout was more than a temporary disruption — it was a stress test for the entire decentralized movement. Blockchain may have freed users from banks and governments in the financial sense, but it still lives within their digital borders.

When your infrastructure is completely centralized:

• Single points of failure
• Limited scalability
• Vendor lock-in
• Higher costs

These are the same issues plaguing traditional systems.

But what if there was an alternative? pic.twitter.com/gEzk4lamqO

— Constantine Zaitcev | dRPC.ORG (@constantine_rm) February 21, 2025

Until the world builds internet infrastructure that is as distributed and censorship-resistant as the blockchains running on top of it, decentralization will remain an unfinished revolution — one that can still be silenced with a single command.

data.title: A detailed analysis of Kohaku's end-to-end privacy solution and user experience
data.description: While the Ethereum Foundation officially announced the Kohaku privacy wallet yesterday, the project had actually been confirmed through various channels as early as June of this year. Kohaku will integrate multiple components across multiple teams to create a comprehensive browser plugin wallet that other wallets can reference, enhancing end-to-end privacy. These include the Helios light client developed by a16z, the Ethereum Foundation's PSE team, as well as Ambire, Railgun, and Wonderland. The origin of the name Kohaku is quite interesting. Because the project forked from Ambire, it chose the Japanese name for Amber: Kohaku. However, Kohaku also means "koi fish" (specifically referring to red and white), which is why the project uses a koi fish as its image.

data.title: Father of Two Stabbed and Robbed of $600K in Bitcoin During Brutal Israeli Home Invasion
data.description:
Brutal Crypto Robbery Shakes Herzliya Resident With $600,000 Stolen

A father of two duaghters in Herzliya, a city on Israel’s central Mediterranean coast near Tel Aviv, faced a terrifying home invasion when three men, led by Murad Mahajna, 46, allegedly ambushed him to seize his cryptocurrency and valuables.

The attack unfolded on September 7, when Mahajna and his accomplices waited in the building’s stairwell at 8:10 a.m. before forcing their way into the apartment once the victim arrived.

The ordeal left the man bound, injured, and forced to hand over hundreds of thousands in cryptocurrency.

Mahajna was formally charged on Wednesday by the Tel Aviv District Attorney’s Office.

(1) An Israeli man has been charged on Wednesday with carrying out a violent crypto robbery in the Israeli city of Herzliya.

Prosecutors in Tel Aviv have filed an indictment against Murad Mahajneh, 46, alleging that he and two unidentified accomplices broke into an apartment… pic.twitter.com/sICbFJHv40

— DL News (@DLNewsInfo) October 9, 2025

Assailants Use Violence to Extract Crypto Wallets

Inside the apartment, two of the attackers wore black masks while Mahajna remained unmasked, claiming,

“We came to take money, we are from the Karaja family.”

Initially, they demanded 500 Bitcoin, roughly $61 million, before turning violent when the victim refused to hand over his digital wallet credentials.

One assailant brandished a kitchen knife at the victim’s neck, and after continued resistance, stabbed him twice above the knees.

Bound and in pain, the victim eventually provided access to his wallets, allowing the attackers to transfer $547,260 in Bitcoin and $42,248 in USDT.

Luxury Items and Cash Also Taken During Hour-Long Ordeal

In addition to cryptocurrency, the group stole a Rolex valued at approximately $50,000, a Trezor hardware wallet, a laptop, about €5,000 in cash, and several thousand shekels.

Before fleeing, they sprayed disinfectant to remove evidence and left the victim tied up, bruised, and bleeding.

Mahajna reportedly threatened, “If the police are contacted, the family will be harmed,” and called the victim two days later demanding more coins, warning, “Wherever you took the wife and children, we will find them.”

Arrest Follows Evidence Collection and Repeat Offender Record

The victim initially hesitated to involve the authorities but eventually confided in family, who persuaded him to report the crime.

Police arrested Mahajna on September 10, relying on security footage, recorded phone calls, voice identification, and technological evidence.

Mahajna faces charges including breaking and entering, aggravated robbery, extortion by threats, obstruction of justice, and making threats.

He has denied the allegations.

Mahajna is a repeat offender with ten prior convictions spanning violent, drug, and weapons-related offences.

Wrench Attacks Are on the Rise With Bitcoin Prices

Security experts warn the incident is part of a growing global trend of “wrench attacks,” where assailants use violence to steal cryptocurrency.

​​

🚨 BREAKING: Two indicted in Tel Aviv over $600,000 ‘wrench attack’ on Bitcoin trader. Global physical attacks on crypto holders hit 52 cases in 2025. #Bitcoin#Crypto

— CryptoAlert (@SatoshiWatch) October 9, 2025

According to blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis, 35 such attacks occurred worldwide in July 2025, coinciding with Bitcoin reaching over $122,000, before breaking $126,000 later.

Jameson Lopp, who tracks these incidents, noted that 2025 could be an all-time high for such attacks, with the Herzliya case marking the 52nd reported incident this year.

Analysts warn that personal wallets are increasingly targeted as attackers focus on individual users rather than large exchanges.

France Emerges as a Global Hotspot for Crypto Violence

France has seen at least ten crypto-related kidnappings since January, including the brutal case of Ledger co-founder David Balland, who was kidnapped and had his finger severed by attackers demanding ransom in cryptocurrency.

Coordinated attacks in May targeted multiple crypto executives and their families, leading prosecutors to charge 25 suspects linked to these abduction plots.

Sophisticated tactics are common, including attackers disguising themselves as delivery workers using branded vans.

One incident involved the kidnapping of a poker player’s father in Paris, with attackers filming mutilation to force a €5-7 million transfer.

🇫🇷 5 suspects arrested for brutal kidnapping of the father of a French cryptocurrency entrepreneur

They sliced off the father's finger, cut him allover his body, and tortured him repeatedly.

“Son of a b**ch, do you think we’re joking? We’re going to cut your fingers off… We… pic.twitter.com/6O4I2E512g

— Remix News & Views (@RMXnews) June 3, 2025

In August, French elite forces rescued a Swiss hostage held near Valence-TGV station after kidnappers demanded cryptocurrency.

🇫🇷🇨🇭 FLASH | Encore un ENLÈVEMENT secoue le secteur de la cryptomonnaie. Un Suisse a été enlevé pendant plusieurs jours puis libéré par les gendarmes près de Valence, qui ont ARRÊTÉ sept individus. Les ravisseurs, qui avaient VIOLENTÉ leur victime, exigeaient une rançon en… pic.twitter.com/XHqrLDAhvW

— Cerfia (@CerfiaFR) September 5, 2025

Global Reach of Violent Crypto Crimes

Similar incidents have occurred worldwide.

In the U.S., two brothers in Texas faced federal kidnapping charges for holding a Minnesota family at gunpoint for nine hours to steal $8 million in crypto.

A violent "crypto-kidnapping" has rocked a Minnesota suburb.

Two brothers from Texas, Raymond & Isiah Garcia, are facing federal charges for allegedly holding a Washington County family hostage at gunpoint for over 9 hours to steal crypto 🪙 :|

The armed home invasion in… pic.twitter.com/q9gV5BMR9d

— Sophia Hodlberg (@sophiaHodlberg) September 29, 2025

Belgium sentenced three men to 12 years for kidnapping a crypto investor’s wife, while an Australian billionaire narrowly escaped kidnapping in Estonia.

The UK also saw a gang sentenced to 13–20 years for torturing a crypto investor and forcing transfers of approximately $124,000.

Experts Warn of Heightened Risks for Crypto Holders

Mehow Pospieszalski, CEO of decentralised wallet platform AmericanFortress, highlighted on the weak cooperation with the crypto community, stating,

“Law enforcement [is] not exactly proactive about protecting founders.”

He advised users to obscure physical data and consider defensive measures, though acknowledged options are limited in most regions.

As Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies continue to surge in value, analysts warn that physical attacks on holders may escalate, signalling a dangerous new frontier in digital asset crime.

data.title: Gemini Launches "Intergalactic Arm" In Australia as U.S. Crypto Rules Stall
data.description:
Gemini Bets on Australia’s Crypto Boom as U.S. Regulatory Uncertainty Persists

Gemini’s newly launched Australian exchange signals the Winklevoss twins’ shifting global strategy — one that embraces regulatory clarity abroad while taking a cautious stance at home.

🇦🇺 Gemini is going Down Under!

The crypto exchange just launched a registered Australian entity with AUSTRAC approval — now offering direct local services instead of global access.

Australia’s crypto scene just got bigger 🦘💥#Gemini#Crypto#Bitcoinpic.twitter.com/70SSmDC9i1

— Crypto Edge (@CryptoEdgeCo) October 10, 2025

Gemini, the crypto exchange founded by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, has officially opened its doors in Australia under a new entity, Gemini Intergalactic Australia, marking a strategic expansion into one of the world’s fastest-growing crypto markets.

The move underscores how U.S. firms are increasingly seeking friendlier jurisdictions amid tightening regulations back home. Registered with the Australian Transaction Reports and Analysis Centre (AUSTRAC), Gemini Intergalactic Australia will now offer locally tailored crypto services to both retail and institutional investors.

The exchange’s Asia-Pacific head, Saad Ahmed , confirmed during Singapore’s TOKEN2049 event that the decision was driven by surging domestic demand.

"We're changing that because we think Australia is a market that we want to be in, and it's a market that we want to grow, so we're going to build a local team here."

Australia’s crypto adoption rate has reached a record high. According to the Independent Reserve Cryptocurrency Index , 31% of Australians now hold digital assets — up from 28% last year — reflecting a steady rise in mainstream acceptance. Gemini’s launch aims to capitalize on this momentum, offering a secure, compliant gateway for investors seeking exposure to digital assets without relying on offshore platforms.

Older Australians (60+) are the fastest-growing #crypto cohort, with a 723% surge in first deposits. #XRP surpassed Bitcoin as most traded asset; #BTC Markets saw 69% climb in #SMSF registrations, widening market share. 📈

Read the full story 👉 https://t.co/V3eTBSITazpic.twitter.com/tVAcJnqrZ4

— Crypto News 🇦🇺 (@cryptonews_aus) October 10, 2025

The exchange’s expansion coincides with regulatory tailwinds in the country. The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) recently introduced temporary relief for intermediaries distributing stablecoins from licensed Australian Financial Services (AFS) providers.

These reforms allow issuers — such as Catena Digital Pty Ltd , creator of the AUDM stablecoin — to operate without additional licensing burdens until 2028. The move signals Canberra’s intent to foster innovation while maintaining oversight.

Shifting Strategy After a Blockbuster U.S. Debut

Gemini’s push into Australia follows its $425 million Nasdaq debut, one of the strongest digital asset listings this year. Shares in Gemini Space Station were priced at $28, above initial expectations, as investor enthusiasm fueled one of crypto’s most successful IPOs in recent memory. Despite going public, the Winklevoss twins retain nearly 94.5% of the firm’s voting power, keeping tight control over strategic direction.

🎙️ @Gemini co-founders @Tyler & @Cameron Winklevoss, reflect on $GEMI’s journey to IPO day. Here’s to the future! #NasdaqListedpic.twitter.com/5mMcB85dhA

— Nasdaq Exchange (@NasdaqExchange) September 12, 2025

While Gemini’s U.S. operations have been vocal in policy debates — including legal challenges against the SEC — its Australian arm is said to be taking a more measured, observant stance. Sources close to the company suggest Gemini will focus on building local market trust before taking any regulatory advocacy role, a contrast to the more politically active posture of its American counterpart.

"We're confident that what ever regulation comes into effect, we'll be well prepared for it and we'll be able to comply with it."

Australia’s crypto scene isn’t without its challenges. Since mid-2023, ASIC has taken down over 14,000 scam and phishing websites, with roughly 20% linked to crypto-related frauds.

The regulator continues to target an estimated 130 new malicious sites weekly, many of which deploy AI-generated ads promising unrealistic crypto profits. This growing threat landscape has intensified demand for trustworthy, compliant exchanges — a space Gemini aims to fill.

🚨🇦🇺 BREAKING: A $123M crypto scam in Australia is one of the nation's largest frauds! Funds were laundered through a licensed money transfer firm, revealing serious gaps in regulatory oversight. #CryptoScam#FraudAlert#Australia#BlockchainSecurity#RegulationReform… pic.twitter.com/fzc13jT2zv

— Crypto News Hunters 🎯 (@CryptoNewsHntrs) July 5, 2025

By securing full AUSTRAC registration, Gemini is signaling to Australian investors that it intends to operate transparently within national guidelines. “We’ve always believed compliance is a competitive advantage,” a Gemini spokesperson noted, emphasizing the company’s long-standing emphasis on security and regulatory cooperation.

A Calculated Bet on Global Diversification

Gemini’s foray into Australia is more than an expansion — it’s a strategic hedge against regulatory uncertainty in the U.S. as Washington’s stance on crypto remains fragmented, Australia offers a clearer, more constructive framework for growth.

🚨SEC CHAIR FINALLY BREAKS SILENCE ON “PROJECT CRYPTO” — MASSIVE REGULATION SHAKE-UP COMING 😱

Paul Atkins just confirmed the U.S. is GEARING UP to REWRITE the RULES for ALL CRYPTO…

and the goal is nothing less than making AMERICA the CRYPTO CAPITAL of the WORLD 🌎💥

Here’s… pic.twitter.com/7e3dflrVFE

— Diana (@InvestWithD) August 15, 2025

By taking a “wait-and-see” approach to Australian regulation while investing in local infrastructure, Gemini is positioning itself to benefit from both prudence and proximity.

If Australia continues to balance innovation with effective oversight, Gemini’s Intergalactic arm could become a model for how American exchanges can thrive abroad without abandoning compliance. In the broader race for crypto’s global future, Gemini’s move Down Under might just prove to be its most grounded decision yet.

data.title: Beneath Human Nature, Above Mechanism: Analyzing the Ecosystem Built by BNB Chain
data.description:
Author: Danny; Source: X, @agintender

The core principle of Chaoshan money houses is to "keep their own money within the system," ensuring absolute internal circulation and value accumulation. BNB Chain's development path and training system also appear to be moving in this direction. The cornerstone of this model is that "from asset issuance to trading, to leverage, everything is integrated within the ecosystem." Binance, through its position as a universal CEX, attracts retail users and capital from around the world, particularly in Asia. Then, through a series of design measures, they ensure that these funds and user activities remain within the BNB Chain ecosystem, which they control to the greatest extent possible, forming a powerful closed value loop: Onboarding (Deposit Attraction): Users deposit funds through Binance CEX, purchase BNB, and participate in Launchpad/Holder airdrops/trading/deposit and withdrawal. Asset Issuance (Value Transfer): Platforms like Four.Meme act as "asset factories," continuously producing new Meme coins and providing speculative targets for funds within the ecosystem. Trading (Exchange/Circulation): PancakeSwap, as the ecosystem's "central marketplace," handles almost all trading needs, with transaction fees and other value captured on-chain. Leverage (Credit Expansion): "Leading" projects like Aster and Lista DAO provide lending and staking services, amplifying the ecosystem's existing capital, further boosting trading activity and accelerating capital circulation. This process is like a bank: after absorbing deposits, instead of investing them in the external market, they lend, invest, and trade internally. All profits and value generated remain within the system, ultimately driving up the value of the entire ecosystem (and $BNB). I. A Three-Tier Funnel Structure for Absolute Internal Circulation and Value Accumulation Tier 1: Traffic Intake and Asset Issuance (Four.Meme) Four.Meme's core role is to serve as a "factory" for traffic intake and asset issuance. Industrialized Asset Issuance Platform: Pump.fun pioneered the meme launch platform model, and BNB Chain has "industrialized" it through platforms like Four.Meme. This means BNB Chain has established an efficient and scalable "production line" capable of continuously creating new meme-coin assets. This continuous "new product launch" capability is key to attracting speculative and high-return retail users, driving a steady stream of traffic and attention to the entire ecosystem. Retail User Capture Engine: BNB Chain's core user profile is a large global retail user base, particularly in the Asian market. Memes' low barrier to entry, high volatility, and community-driven nature perfectly align with the preferences of this user group. Therefore, supporting platforms like Four.Meme is equivalent to mastering the core engine for attracting and converting these users. Layer 2: Fair Prices and Value Anchoring (Aspecta.ai) As a price oracle (one of Aspecta's features), Aspecta.ai serves as the "yardstick" of the entire DeFi ecosystem. All leverage and lending activities (such as those conducted on Lista) rely on fair prices provided by the oracle to calculate collateralization ratios and trigger liquidations. If leverage is the "accelerator," then the price oracle is the "dashboard" and "brake." By supporting its own oracle, Binance ensures the reliability and consistency of the ecosystem's most important fundamental data sources, avoiding the risks of data manipulation and single points of failure associated with reliance on external oracles. This is like having a bank with its own dedicated and trusted asset appraiser, ensuring the accuracy of all collateral valuations, which is the cornerstone of the stability of the entire credit system. Layer 3: Core Leverage and Financial Infrastructure (Aster, PancakeSwap, Lista) If Four.Meme is the "front door" attracting users, then Aster, PancakeSwap, and Lista are the "core halls" that carry these users and funds into deep economic activity. PancakeSwap: Liquidity and Trading Core As the dominant DEX on BNB Chain, PancakeSwap is the liquidity center of the entire ecosystem. All assets issued through Four.Meme ultimately need to be traded and provide liquidity on PancakeSwap. It is not only a trading venue but also a key indicator of ecosystem activity. Controlling PancakeSwap is equivalent to controlling the lifeblood of asset pricing and liquidity within the ecosystem. Aster Dex & Lista DAO: Direct Leverage Providers Aster Dex is a rapidly growing decentralized contract trading platform. Its total value locked (TVL) has surged 570% in a short period of time, reaching $2.34 billion. This, combined with recent incentivized trading activity, has directly driven growth in the number of active addresses on BNB Chain. Lista DAO Lending is the most direct and core leverage tool in DeFi. Lista provides financial support to the ecosystem by offering the stablecoin lisUSD. Stablecoins are the foundational building blocks of DeFi, enabling all complex financial activities such as lending, leveraged trading, and derivatives.

II. Wealth Amplification Cycle: The Complete Path from $1 to $n

This cycle clearly demonstrates how various products work together and interconnectedly to amplify initial capital. (Taking revolving loans as an example)

Step 1: Invest

  • You have $1 and are attracted to Four.Meme's new project.

  • You use this $1 to purchase the meme asset you're interested in on PancakeSwap.

Step 2: Collateral

  • Deposit your Meme assets (or other assets such as BNB) into Lista DAO or Aster as collateral.

Step 3: Valuation

  • Aspecta.ai (price oracle) immediately provides a fair valuation of the collateral and tells Lista/Aster how much your assets are worth and how much you can borrow.

Step 4: Borrow

  • Based on Aspecta.ai's valuation, successfully borrow a certain amount of stablecoin lisUSD from Lista DAO (for example, worth $0.6).

Step 5: Reinvest/Leverage

  • Reinvest the borrowed lisUSD stablecoins into PancakeSwap/Aster to purchase more of the same Meme asset, open contracts, or invest in other promising new assets.

  • Result: You initially only invested $1 in principal, but now hold assets worth $1.6. You have completed your first leverage. (Aster can amplify even more.)

Loop ➔$n

  • As long as the price of the collateralized asset rises, the borrowable amount increases. Participants can repeat steps 2-5, continuously expanding their positions, eventually theoretically increasing the initial $1 to $n (while also amplifying the risk).

This three-layer structure creates a powerful flywheel effect:

Four.Meme (entry) ➔ PancakeSwap/Aster (trading) ➔ Aster/Lista (leverage) ➔ PancakeSwap (reinvestment)

Aspecta.ai is the "value cornerstone" that runs through it all, ensuring the entire leverage system does not collapse. Through this sophisticated collaborative design, BNB Chain has created a highly efficient capital amplification machine for retail users.

Third, Strategic Intention: Why is it necessary to control leverage in our own hands?

Binance cultivates these projects as its "direct descendants" because it must maintain control over the core financial rights of the ecosystem—especially leverage—in its own hands. Risk Management and System Stability: "Experiences of a margin call are prone to accidents" are key. Leverage is a double-edged sword: it can accelerate prosperity, but it can also trigger chain liquidations during market downturns, leading to systemic collapse. By supporting "indigenous" lending, stablecoin, and contract trading projects with leveraged properties like Aster and Lista, Binance can gain a deeper understanding of the ecosystem's leverage levels and risk exposure. This gives it the ability to intervene and coordinate in extreme situations, preventing the collapse of third-party protocols from causing devastating damage to the entire BNB Chain. Accelerating the Flywheel Effect: Leverage is an "amplifier" of ecosystem growth. Users can pledge their assets on Lista, borrow stablecoins, and then purchase new meme coins or other assets on PancakeSwap. Alternatively, they can open nx leveraged positions on Aster. This process greatly improves capital efficiency, creates more purchasing power out of thin air, and thus drives up trading volume, TVL, and network activity. This enables the "Binance flywheel" to spin at an even faster pace, further enhancing the ecosystem's prosperity. Closed Value Loop: By ensuring that core processes like asset issuance, trading, and lending are completed by "independent" projects within the ecosystem, Binance ensures that the vast majority of value (such as transaction fees and lending interest) is retained within the BNB Chain, ultimately feeding back into the value of $BNB, forming a closed business loop. Binance's strategy is to build a vertically integrated financial ecosystem: Using Four.Meme as the engine for asset issuance, it continuously generates the "fuel" to attract retail users. Then, through core infrastructure such as PancakeSwap, Aster, and Lista, it provides these users and funds with a platform for trading, accumulating funds, and leveraging them, thereby maximizing the ecosystem's economic vitality and system stability.

Fourth. Centralized Trust and the Role of the "Patriarch"

Underground banks rely not on external laws but on the credibility of key individuals. The operation of BNB Chain is also highly dependent on the implicit endorsement and strong intervention of Binance, the "patriarch."

BNB Chain's degree of decentralization has been criticized, citing its small number of validator nodes and perceived close ties to Binance. However, this "centralization" has, to some extent, become an advantage in attracting retail investors:

  • Reputation as a guarantee: Projects choose to develop on BNB Chain, and users choose to trade on it, largely out of trust in the "Binance" brand. An investment from Binance Labs or selection for the MVB program is like a banker's stamp of approval on a project, significantly reducing the cost of trust for users. Forceful intervention to ensure safety: The most typical example is the 2022 BNB Chain suspension of the entire chain due to a cross-chain bridge attack. From the perspective of decentralized fundamentalism, this is unacceptable. However, from the perspective of bankers, this is the "family head" taking drastic measures to protect "family" assets in times of crisis and prevent runs and collapse. This practice sacrifices the principle of decentralization while catering to the simple needs of retail investors for financial security. V. High Efficiency, Low Barriers, and Specific Community Services Underground banks exist because they are faster, more flexible, and more understanding of the needs of their own people than traditional banks. BNB Chain's success also stems from its precise targeting of a specific demographic: cost-sensitive global retail traders. Extreme cost and speed advantages: During the 2020 DeFi summer, BNB Chain offered significantly lower transaction costs and faster confirmation times compared to Ethereum's high gas fees. This is crucial for meme/DeFi operations, which require high-frequency, small-volume, and rapid inflows and outflows. It's like a money exchange, bypassing the cumbersome banking process and providing the most direct and efficient service. Serving a specific cultural community: BNB Chain's rise is closely tied to the participation of Asian communities, particularly the Chinese-speaking community. It has successfully provided a "perfect breeding ground" for the explosion of Chinese memes, precisely catering to the needs of this vast community, from cultural resonance (such as the "Binance Life" meme) to trading habits. This is much like the Chaoshan money exchange serving the Chaoshan merchant community, with a natural foundation of culture and trust.

Summary

Comparing the BNB Chain model to a "Chaoshan underground bank" isn't a derogatory term, but rather an insight into the essence of its business model. It reveals a truth: BNB Chain's prosperity isn't the result of a purely decentralized, bottom-up, organic ecosystem, but rather a highly efficient on-chain economy meticulously orchestrated and tightly controlled by Binance, achieving an internal circulation of capital and value.

While this model deviates somewhat from the decentralized model of the crypto world, it is undoubtedly extremely successful commercially. It meets the core needs of the vast majority of retail users in the most pragmatic way: low cost, high efficiency, and the trust and reliance on a trusted advisor in a chaotic market.

Below human nature, above mechanisms—that's life.
